prompt: Implement variational (locked) dropout as a torch.nn.Module class `VariationalDropout`. Constructor: VariationalDropout(p: float = 0.5). Forward takes a tensor of shape (B, L, D). In training mode (`self.training == True`), sample ONE dropout mask of shape (B, 1, D) and broadcast it across the sequence length L (same mask for every position in the sequence). Scale by 1/(1-p) like standard inverted dropout. In eval mode (`self.training == False`), pass the input through unchanged.

criteria.description: All pytest tests in unit_tests pass against the candidate implementation

unit_tests:
import torch
from solution import VariationalDropout

def test_eval_mode_is_identity():
    drop = VariationalDropout(p=0.5)
    drop.eval()
    x = torch.randn(2, 4, 8)
    out = drop(x)
    assert torch.allclose(out, x, atol=1e-6)

def test_train_mode_mask_is_locked_across_sequence():
    torch.manual_seed(0)
    drop = VariationalDropout(p=0.5)
    drop.train()
    x = torch.ones(4, 7, 16)
    out = drop(x)
    # Every position along seq L must have the same per-batch-per-dim mask.
    pos0 = out[:, 0, :]
    for t in range(1, 7):
        assert torch.allclose(out[:, t, :], pos0, atol=1e-6), t

def test_p_zero_is_identity_even_in_training():
    drop = VariationalDropout(p=0.0)
    drop.train()
    x = torch.randn(2, 4, 8)
    out = drop(x)
    assert torch.allclose(out, x, atol=1e-6)

def test_invalid_p_raises():
    import pytest
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        VariationalDropout(p=1.0)
